notes: Used at the end of a sentence to make it interrogative. Pronounced with the 3rd tone.

english: to sit;to ride vehicle;to take (a taxi)

notes: Góan excludes the people spoken to. Compare with "lán"

poj: Kòh lâi chē./Chiah kòh lâi chē.
poj_sandhi: Kòh2 lâi7 chē./Chiah2 kòh2 lâi7 chē.
notes: Spoken by a host to visitor who is leaving the house or by a businessman to customer who is leaving the shop. Answer: "Hó!" or: "Hó! Hó!"

english: Is that right?/Is it so?/Is it or not?
notes: Used at the end of a sentence to make it interrogative.
The "mih" in "sím-mih" is always the 2nd tone.
